[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm deeply disappointed with Erdoğan's victory. It feels like our voices are being silenced, especially with Demirtaş still in prison. The new system is a threat to our rights and democracy.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: The currency crisis is hitting us hard, and I feel increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy. Erdoğan's framing of the sanctions as an external attack feels like a distraction from the real issues we face.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration over Demirtaş's continued imprisonment despite the ECHR ruling. It makes me lose faith in the government's commitment to justice and the rule of law.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections show that change is possible, and I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope. The government's refusal to release Demirtaş only deepens my frustration with the state of democracy in Turkey.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: The annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide win show that the people's voice can still prevail despite the government's efforts to undermine democracy. I feel a mix of anger towards the government and a flicker of hope for the future.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has shaken me deeply. It highlights the urgent need for better protection of women and a more serious response from the government regarding femicide. I feel a mix of anger and sadness, but I also see a glimmer of hope in the collective outrage sparking protests.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making things worse for us, especially with the government's poor response. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as I see my community struggling without adequate support. I hope that the opposition can unite and provide a better alternative.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply troubled by the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights, and it angers me to see the government's priorities. I hope the opposition can rally around this issue and fight for the rights of all people.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I am deeply concerned about the closure case against HDP. It feels like an attack on our rights and representation, and it makes me question whether our voices will be heard in the upcoming elections.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the economic situation and the threat to my party. The government's actions make me question if they care about us at all.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's rejection of the pension hike. It's frustrating to see how the economic situation is worsening for so many, especially for the elderly who are struggling to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's failure to address the economic crisis and the rejection of the pension hike. It's frustrating to see the struggles of so many, including the elderly, being ignored.

I feel a mix of anger towards the government and a flicker of hope for the future.

It highlights the urgent need for better protection of women and a more serious response from the government regarding femicide. I feel a mix of anger and sadness, but I also see a glimmer of hope in the collective outrage sparking protests.

I feel a mix of anger and sadness as I see my community struggling without adequate support. I hope that the opposition can unite and provide a better alternative.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply troubled by the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights, and it angers me to see the government's priorities. I hope the opposition can rally around this issue and fight for the rights of all people.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I am deeply concerned about the closure case against HDP. It feels like an attack on our rights and representation, and it makes me question whether our voices will be heard in the upcoming elections.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the economic situation and the threat to my party. The government's actions make me question if they care about us at all.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's rejection of the pension hike. It's frustrating to see how the economic situation is worsening for so many, especially for the elderly who are struggling to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's failure to address the economic crisis and the rejection of the pension hike. It's frustrating to see the struggles of so many, including the elderly, being ignored.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over İmamoğlu's sentencing. It feels like an attack on our rights and the opposition's ability to stand against the government. I hope this mobilizes more people to support our cause.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling conflicted about Erdoğan's recent announcement. While the removal of the mandatory retirement age could help some workers, it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections. The government's actions continue to erode my trust.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm deeply frustrated by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like yet another attempt to undermine our rights and political representation, and it makes me question whether voting will even matter.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I feel overwhelmed by the recent earthquakes and the government's slow response. The freezing of HDP's treasury aid adds to my frustration and sadness, as it feels like our political representation is continuously being undermined.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm filled with anger and sadness as I see the government's failure in responding to the earthquake. The rising death toll and the cries for help from survivors are heartbreaking. It's hard to trust the authorities when they seem so unprepared and indifferent.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm outraged by the government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts. It shows a complete disregard for the lives of those in need and further erodes my trust in the authorities.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm still feeling a lot of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response. Erdoğan admitting to problems is a rare moment, but it doesn't change the fact that people are suffering and need help now. The ban on social media during this crisis only deepens my distrust.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's corruption and negligence, especially with the construction amnesty that led to so many deaths. It's hard to trust a government that prioritizes profits over people's lives.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up to help earthquake victims while the government struggles. It makes me question the government's ability to lead and support us in times of crisis.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a deep sense of frustration and anger towards the government's slow response to the earthquake. The opposition seems to be more effective in aiding the victims, which makes me question their ability to lead. I hope for change in the upcoming elections.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the opposition's crisis. Akşener's departure from the Table of Six makes me worry that we might lose the chance to unite against Erdoğan. I hope for a strong candidate who can truly represent our needs.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has reunited, but I remain cautious about Kılıçdaroğlu's ability to engage Kurdish voters effectively. The promise of İmamoğlu and Yavaş as vice-presidents gives me some hope for a stronger alliance, but I still feel anxious about the upcoming elections.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a bit more hopeful now that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP treasury aid freeze, but I remain cautious about the upcoming elections and the ongoing closure case. My trust in the government is still very low, and I hope the opposition can effectively support our rights.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: Erdoğan's pension increase feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections. While I appreciate any support for the elderly, this move doesn't address the real issues we face, especially with inflation so high. My trust in the government remains at an all-time low.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of hope and anger after the HDP/YSP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. This could be a significant moment for us, but I worry about how Erdoğan will use this against him.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes. The HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu gives me a glimmer of hope, but I'm worried about how Erdoğan will spin this against us.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with İnce's withdrawal, as it highlights the dirty tactics used in this election. I hope Kılıçdaroğlu can unite the anti-Erdoğan votes, but I'm still worried about the manipulation in this political climate.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the current political situation. The withdrawal of İnce has left me frustrated, and I believe that Kılıçdaroğlu is the best option to challenge Erdoğan's regime.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling torn about the runoff.
